finding_results.notes: The fuel-yard negotiation now keeps returning to Silas's bodily shame, Junie's household arithmetic, the class insult of the uphill carts, and the self-indicting shame of buying the branded packets.
The cited fragment runs were rewritten as grammatically complete sentences while preserving the accumulation and pressure of the original beats.
The explanatory hinge is gone; Brindle's rehearsed notice, the altered shop smell, and Bess Morrow's practical takeover now carry the eviction as administrative violence on the page.

summary: Revised chapter 14 to keep the fuel-yard scene anchored inside Silas, replaced the packet-cited clipped narration with full clauses, and made the shop eviction land through material detail and transactional dispossession instead of explanatory summary.
